certificate.moduleName: Arista Crypto Module v3.0 [Software, Software IPsec, Web Portal]
certificate.caveat: Interim validation. When operated in approved mode. No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs (e.g., keys)
certificate.description: This validation is for the library contained within the CloudVision Portal (CVP) products and all its related SKUs, which includes SS-CV-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-SWITCH-1M-P, SS-CV-MOD-G-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-G-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-LT-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-LT-MOD-G-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-LT-G-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-SWITCH-LAB-1M, SS-CV-ENT-1M, SS-CV-ENT-1M-P, SS-CV-T1-1M, SS-CV-T2-1M, SS-CV-LT-T1-1M, SS-CV-LT-T2-1M, SS-CV-G-T1-1M, SS-CV-G-T2-1M, SS-CV-LT-G-T1-1M, SS-CV-LT-G-T2-1M, SS-CV-MOD-G-T1-1M, SS-CV-MOD-G-T2-1M, SS-CV-LT-MOD-G-T1-1M, SS-CV-LT-MOD-G-T2-1M, SS-CV-ENT-1M-P, SS-CV-CG-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-CG-T1-1M, SS-CV-CG-T2-1M, SS-CV-LT-CG-SWITCH-1M, SS-CV-LT-CG-T1-1M, SS-CV-LT-CG-T2-1M and any other future SKUs which use the validated library for the CloudVision Portal (CVP) product.
testedConfigurations: CloudVision Portal 2023.2.0 on VMware ESXi 6.7.0 on CentOS 7 running on a Supermicro SYS-6029TP-HTR with Intel Xeon Gold 5218R with PAA CloudVision Portal 2023.2.0 on VMware ESXi 6.7.0 on CentOS 7 running on a Supermicro SYS-6029TP-HTR with Intel Xeon Gold 5218R without PAA

rows: Name: Arista Crypto Module v3.0 [Software, Software IPsec, Web Portal] Version: 3.0
CloudVision Portal 2023.2.0 on VMware ESXi 6.7.0 on CentOS 7
CloudVision Portal 2023.2.0 on VMware ESXi 6.7.0 on CentOS 7
Any compatible OS with any virtualization software
Single Approved Mode – selected by calling the FIPS_mode_set( 1) function.
The status indicator is a return value 1 from the FIPS_mode() function.
Selected by default in CloudVision Portal
The status indicator is a return value 0 from the FIPS_mode() function.
Authenticated Encrypt, Authenticated Decrypt, Message Authentication
Confidentiality on storage devices only [XTS-AES is compliant to IG C.I by checking for Key_1 ≠ Key_2.]
Deterministic Random Bit Generation [Module defaults to Counter DRBG with 256- bit security strength]
Curve: P-256, P-384, P-521; Hash Algorithm: SHA2-224, SHA2-256, SHA2-384, SHA2-512
Curve: P-256, P-384, P-521; Hash Algorithm: SHA-1, SHA2- 224, SHA2-256, SHA2-384, SHA2-512
Message Authentication, KDF primitive, integrity test
Key Agreement [Relies on calling application to feed shared secret into KDF

The module generates a new key and tests the key via a PCT. If the test fails, an error is returned.
Error message is placed into the error queue and an error is returned from the API.
The module is aborted – restart module
Error message is output on stderr.

Name of the Arista operating system
Vendor Affirmed cryptographic algorithms are Approved algorithms for which no CAVP tests are available yet. The vendor performs their own testing as the basis for their affirmation.
